name: Northwest, Inc. v. Ginsberg
summary: Held that the ADA preempted a state implied-covenant-of-good-faith claim because it related to the airline's frequent-flyer program, a 'service' under the statute.
significance: Further elaborated on the breadth of ADA preemption in the airline context and the distinction between generally applicable background law and targeted regulation, relevant to whether common-law negligence is preempted.
